steps.action: The Kalamatianós (Greek: Καλαματιανός, pronounce with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: The Kalamatianós (Greek: Καλαματιανός, pronounce with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: It is a popular Greek folk dance throughout Gree
steps.text: It is a popular Greek folk dance throughout Greece, Cyprus and internationally and is often performed at many social gatherings worldwide.

steps.action: As is the case with most Greek folk dances, it i
steps.text: As is the case with most Greek folk dances, it is danced in chain with a counterclockwise rotation, the dancers holding hands.

steps.action: It is a joyous and festive dance; its musical be
steps.text: It is a joyous and festive dance; its musical beat is 78, subdivided into of three parts of 3+2+2 beats, corresponding to 3 steps per bar.

steps.action: There are 12 steps in the dance corresponding to
steps.text: There are 12 steps in the dance corresponding to 4 bars of music.

steps.action: These steps include 10 steps counterclockwise ("
steps.text: These steps include 10 steps counterclockwise ("forward") followed by 2 steps clockwise ("backwards").

steps.action: Depending on the occasion and the dancers' profi
steps.text: Depending on the occasion and the dancers' proficiency, certain steps may be taken as jumps or squats.

steps.action: The lead dancer usually holds the second dancer
steps.text: The lead dancer usually holds the second dancer by a handkerchief, thus allowing them to perform more elaborate steps and acrobatics.

steps.action: The steps of the Kalamatianós are the same as th
steps.text: The steps of the Kalamatianós are the same as those of the Syrtos, but the latter is slower and more stately, its beat being an even 44.

steps.action: Homer, in the Iliad, describes three performance
steps.text: Homer, in the Iliad, describes three performances made around the spear of Achilles that depict a dance in an open circle.

steps.action: The ancient Spartans had a dance called ὅρμος hó
steps.text: The ancient Spartans had a dance called ὅρμος hórmos, which was a syrto-style dance described in detail by Xenophon where a woman led a male into dance using a handkerchief.

steps.action: Lucian states that the hórmos dance was performe
steps.text: Lucian states that the hórmos dance was performed in an open circle and was done by young men and women.

steps.action: The men would dance vigorously while the women d
steps.text: The men would dance vigorously while the women danced with modest movements.

steps.action: In the 19th century, this dance was called Συρτό
steps.text: In the 19th century, this dance was called Συρτός ὁ Πελοποννήσιος Syrtós ho Peloponnḗsios.

steps.action: It is believed to have acquired the name kalamat
steps.text: It is believed to have acquired the name kalamatianos from the town of Kalamata in southern Greece; most Greek dances are commonly named after the villages or areas from which they are considered to have originated.

steps.action: Donald Swann used the rhythm of the Kalamatianos
steps.text: Donald Swann used the rhythm of the Kalamatianos (acknowledged in the score) in the fourth movement of his Requiem for the Living.

steps.action: The 1960s popular singer, formerly known as Cat
steps.text: The 1960s popular singer, formerly known as Cat Stevens, wrote and performed a song, Ruby Love, in 78 time with a distinctive Greek flavour.

steps.action: Οι Ελληνικοί Χοροί [Greek Dances].
steps.text: Οι Ελληνικοί Χοροί [Greek Dances].

steps.action: Traditional Dance in Greek in Greek Culture.
steps.text: Traditional Dance in Greek in Greek Culture.

steps.action: An especially haunting example of the kalamatian
steps.text: An especially haunting example of the kalamatianos, Mekapses Yitonissa (Μέκαψες Γειτόνισσα), was recorded for the National Geographic Society's groundbreaking Music of Greece album, released in 1968.

steps.action: The Kalamatiano is played at special occasions,
steps.text: The Kalamatiano is played at special occasions, such as Easter and weddings.

steps.action: Stevens, who would later convert to Islam, has p
steps.text: Stevens, who would later convert to Islam, has paternal Greek Cypriot roots.

steps.action: I come and go, but cannot find you I try your do
steps.text: I come and go, but cannot find you I try your door, and it's locked.

steps.action: Your windows are always lighted I ask your door,
steps.text: Your windows are always lighted I ask your door, "Where has your lady gone?
